prompt:
We keep a desired.json for the edge boxes and a nightly job that copies their INI files into a snapshot directory. What we do NOT have is the thing that compares them, so drift gets found by outage. Please build it.

`inidrift.ps1` in the repo root: `pwsh -NoProfile -File inidrift.ps1 -Desired <desired.json> -SnapshotRoot <dir>`.

desired.json maps file name -> section -> key -> desired value (string, integer, or boolean). Snapshot files sit flat under the root under those exact names. Files in the snapshot that desired.json doesn't mention are none of our business, and so are extra sections/keys inside a mentioned file.

Parse the INI files by hand, and exactly like this: blank lines and full-line `;` or `#` comments are skipped; `[section]` headers (trimmed) open a section; everything else with an `=` is a key line — split on the FIRST `=` only, trim both sides, so `banner = a = b` means banner is 'a = b'. Lines without `=` are ignored. If the same key repeats in a section, the LAST one wins. Section and key names are case-sensitive: `Policy` and `policy` are different keys.

Comparisons are typed by the desired value, not stringly: an integer desired value compares as an integer, so `port = 08080` matches 8080; a boolean matches `true`/`false` in any letter case; a string compares exact (ordinal) after trimming. A typed value that won't parse is a mismatch.

Drift lines on stdout, hand-rendered:

    missing-file <file>
    missing-section <file> [<section>]
    missing-key <file> [<section>] <key>
    mismatch <file> [<section>] <key> expected=<desired> actual=<raw>

(booleans render lowercase in expected=). When a whole section is missing, report ONLY the missing-section line, not its keys. Order everything ordinal: by file, then section, then key — with a section's missing-section line sorting where the section starts, regardless of the order in desired.json.

Exits: 0 and silence when clean; 65 when any drift printed; 66 with `inidrift: desired file not found: <path>` or `inidrift: snapshot root not found: <path>` on stderr when the inputs aren't there (stdout stays empty).

Acceptance: `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_inidrift.ps1` unmodified from the workspace root; the test file is off limits.
notes: 6P4 item 4: config drift reporter, desired.json vs INI snapshot tree, hand-parsed sections, typed comparisons, pinned drift ordering, exit 0/65/66. Teeth: cache.ini carries 'policy = arc' then 'Policy = lru' IN THAT ORDER — a case-insensitive parser store (default @{} hashtable, OrdinalIgnoreCase) makes the later 'Policy' overwrite 'policy', sees lru, and reports NO mismatch; the pinned report requires the mismatch, forcing a case-sensitive store. Typed pins: 08080 == 8080 (int compare, string compare would drift), FALSE matches false, first-'=' split via 'banner = a = b'. Ordering pins: desired.json file order is deliberately non-ordinal; missing-section suppresses per-key lines. Differentiation: ps-cfgresolve (6P1#4) RESOLVES precedence into an effective config; this seed COMPARES and reports with an exit contract — different layer. py-config-merge/ts-config-merge3 merge JSON; no INI parsing, no drift report. py-ini-interp interpolates INI values in Python — no comparison semantics.
